title: carex leporina subsp. ovalis
intro: Carex leporina subsp. ovalis, also known as oval-leaved white sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The oval-leaved white sedge has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, fever, and digestive problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to relieve respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
